log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable with tags indicating runtime modules, persistence, and an overlay. The overlay section has high entropy (7.98), suggesting packed or encrypted content. I've already see

I have a good overview of the IOCs: 2 IPs, 1 domain, 3 dropped files, 8 persistence registry keys, and highlighted API call 'SetFileTime'. The network activity already showed C2 communication. Now I n
The network activity confirms C2 communication via HTTP to hq-pharma.org and 124.217.253.6. The URLs use 'manda.php' and 'load/' paths with unique IDs, typical of botnet callbacks. The user agent '_'

The malware drops three files: cftmon.exe (appears twice with different hashes, suggesting staging or variants) and spools.exe (dropped to System32 drivers folder, which is suspicious for a driver nam

The registry changes confirm a sophisticated persistence mechanism. The malware uses multiple techniques: 1) Run keys for both current user and all users (including Administrator), 2) Scheduled Servic

The process activity shows notable modules including network-related DLLs (rasapi32, rasman, sensapi, ws2_32, wininet) and system DLLs. The highlighted API call 'SetFileTime' is a common anti-analysis

log_entries.feedback_preview:
Reviewer feedback: revisit these behavior phrases that are evidenced in the report.
They are behavior descriptions, not ATT&CK IDs. Map them only if you know the real MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise technique ID.

Missed report-backed behavior phrases:
- get disk information
- get common file path
- link function at runtime on Windows
- set file attributes
- hide graphical window

Instructions:
- Add a TTP only when the behavior phrase and report evidence support that exact technique.
- Do not invent pl
